verdict_reasoning: B2M is the invariant light chain of MHC class I. It has no membrane anchor of its own but assembles intracellularly with the MHC-I heavy chain (HLA-A/B/C) and is co-trafficked to the plasma membrane as a stable non-covalent heterodimer displayed on virtually all nucleated cells. The B2M protein body is exposed on the outer leaflet and is a validated surface target (e.g., anti-B2M antibodies, CAR-T approaches). Note: this is accessibility of the B2M protein body itself via its MHC-I partner, distinct from pMHC peptide presentation.
